sqlite_absolute_rejection.statement: SQLite is absolutely rejected for Handshake; CKC SQLite references are source evidence only and must not be inherited.

source_anchors.signal: Current spec entry records WP-0122..WP-0137 review-batch hardening and code-truth consistency across code, manual, WPs, taskboard, and tests.
Governance and verification rules require LLM visibility, parallel-operation audit, viewport captures, current value/reset visibility, and sample-corpus use.
Intake, Inbox, Collections, contact sheets, sidecars, PoseKit contexts, OpenPose sidecars, parallel editing, and recoverable deletion are current CKC behavior.
Manual/automation hardening and PostgreSQL-compatible regression tests are part of the latest hardening gate.
Non-negotiables include byte-for-byte user text preservation, adult/explicit fields first-class, template integrity, same-line descriptors, minimal UI, and 2-panel/3-panel layouts.
PostgreSQL is CKC's default/current provider; SQLite remains only as CKC legacy/test fallback.
CKC has automation/debugger IPC, internal manual formats, multi-agent sessions/leases, hidden background startup, renderer/backend commands, and file capture.
Intake Sorter supports folder-only and linked CKC profile modes, pending review, and linked metadata writeback.
CKC libraryRoot contains legacy/test SQLite file path only when explicitly configured; PostgreSQL lives outside libraryRoot.
Next-generation global search requirements mention SQLite FTS5; this is CKC evidence only and must translate to non-SQLite Handshake search/indexing.
Taskboard is CKC authoritative work status and must be read with CKC governance surfaces; no product-doc mirror.
WP-0119 through WP-0137 show PostgreSQL-first/SQLite-removal planning, review-batch work, one blocked PoseKit interaction item, and raster contact sheet planning.
Current focus records WP-0137 in REVIEW, full test pass, GUI/sample-corpus review evidence still pending, plus open findings and deferred gates.

spec_heading_inventory.summary: CKC spec combines recent changelog entries, normative sections, roadmap requirements, and appendix session dump.
relevant_headings: Intake, Inbox, Collections, Contact Sheets, And Sidecars

capability_clusters.handshake_fold_in_implication: Fold into Handshake as governance requirements for agent-readable receipts, visual verification evidence, source-backed research summaries, and taskboard/refinement traceability.
Translate to Handshake diagnostics and operator/model control surfaces; preserve consistency-test concept without importing CKC API shape.
Useful for Handshake Atelier/Lens intake lanes, review queues, and recoverability; storage must use Handshake data contracts and PostgreSQL/EventLedger only.

requirement_signals: collections with notes, tags, and links
capability_clusters.handshake_fold_in_implication: Fold as evidence for collection artifacts and preview/export surfaces; translate storage/export to Handshake ArtifactStore.

requirement_signals: blank, single, and collection workbench contexts
capability_clusters.handshake_fold_in_implication: Relevant to Atelier/Lens visual and pose workbench; create Handshake-native interaction WPs only after consolidation and research.
Strong fit for Handshake EventLedger and CRDT/workspace contracts; reuse policy shape, not CKC table/API details.

requirement_signals: SQLite only as CKC legacy/test fallback
capability_clusters.handshake_fold_in_implication: Handshake must go further: SQLite is forbidden everywhere, including tests, fixtures, mocks, examples, caches, compatibility adapters, temporary harnesses, imports, exports, and product paths.
Relevant to Atelier/Lens usability and data preservation; translate into Handshake UX acceptance criteria and source-preservation tests.

status_counts.interpretation: Historical CKC features marked complete; evidence of shipped or intended delivered behavior.
WP-0122..WP-0137 review-batch items implemented but not final DONE evidence.
WP-0133 PoseKit calibration, markers, and history has unresolved interaction work.
WP-0118 prompt-response matrix is parked concept work.
Planned or low-priority future work remains, including WP-0114, WP-0116, WP-0117, WP-0119, and WP-0136.

delivery_signals: WP-0137 is in REVIEW with product/manual dispatch drift fixed, PostgreSQL-compatible backend regression, current spec v00.075, and full npm test pass of 206 passed and 1 skipped.
WP-0122..WP-0135 implementation pass is complete but review evidence remains before DONE promotion.
Raster contact sheet export is planned as WP-0136, not shipped in current CKC evidence.
PostgreSQL-first testing and SQLite removal/quarantine are planned in CKC WP-0119 and reinforced as a current rule.
Open minor findings remain for React 19 Save button click behavior and occluded operator-mode captures.
Deferred validation remains for packaged smoke, NAS mirror backup, DB-backed suite verification against live PostgreSQL, and validation pass for WP-0092 through WP-0095.

unresolved_work: CKC review-batch GUI/sample-corpus captures still need review evidence before DONE promotion.
WP-0133 deeper PoseKit interaction model remains blocked: draggable calibration overlay, missing-marker placement flow, 3D/live split editing, and forked history.
WP-0136 raster contact sheet export is planned and not yet delivered.
WP-0119 indicates CKC still has SQLite remediation work outstanding.
Historical roadmap/spec sections still contain SQLite-centric implementation language, especially global search FTS5 and planned cache/index fields.
Older delivery gates remain deferred or pending, including packaged smoke and DB-backed verification against a live PostgreSQL container.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: CKC mixes delivered, review, blocked, planned, and appendix/history requirements in one spec.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Treat CKC as evidence; require Handshake work packets to cite taskboard status and classify evidence maturity before implementation.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: CKC contains SQLite language in current and historical sections.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Enforce the absolute SQLite rejection in Handshake packet acceptance criteria and validation grep checks.
Translate capability intent into Handshake-native diagnostics, EventLedger receipts, and governed command surfaces.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Review-batch evidence is not all DONE.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use review-batch items as strong but non-final evidence; require fresh Handshake verification rather than importing CKC closure state.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: PoseKit calibration/history is blocked in CKC.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Split Handshake pose/workbench interaction scope into dedicated research-backed work rather than folding blocked CKC behavior silently.

handshake_fold_in_implications: Preserve CKC capability intent, not CKC implementation shape.
Translate storage and collaboration to PostgreSQL, EventLedger, ArtifactStore, CRDT/workspace contracts, and existing governed runtime boundaries.
SQLite rejection must be a hard gate in downstream Handshake packets and validators.
Use CKC taskboard statuses as evidence maturity signals: DONE, REVIEW, BLOCKED, PLANNED, and CONCEPT carry different weights.
Do not create CKC rebuild stubs from this inventory.

High-ROI additions: add a Handshake no-SQLite validator, add evidence-maturity fields to consolidation JSON, and require future fold-in packets to cite CKC source anchors plus Handshake ownership boundaries.
